rag_rules: Generate assume/guarantee clauses only for visible AADL features or explicitly declared eq/const helpers; never reference AADL properties directly.
Place component-type annexes for input/output envelope contracts; use implementation annexes only for assign clauses on existing outputs.
generic_rules: Use eq to derive boolean conditions from numeric ranges before applying them in guarantees.
Declare constants for hardcoded bounds to improve clarity and type safety.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE types are int, real, bool — never integer, boolean, or AADL unit tokens.
Guarantees must reference typed expressions; avoid unbound or undeclared identifiers.
Assign statements are valid only in implementation annexes and require a valid output target.
anti_patterns: Do not use AADL property names (e.g., Period, Compute_Execution_Time) as AGREE variables.
Do not place guarantee clauses in implementation annexes — use assign instead for output binding.
